attributes.magic: PE32 executable (GUI) Intel 80386, for MS Windows

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects specific process characteristics of Maze ransomware word document droppers

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Shadow Copies Deletion Using Operating Systems Utilities
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Shadow Copies deletion using operating systems utilities
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Michael Haag, Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community, Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Uncommon File Created In Office Startup Folder
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the creation of a file with an uncommon extension in an Office application startup folder

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Potential Command Line Path Traversal Evasion Attempt
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects potential evasion or obfuscation attempts using bogus path traversal via the commandline
A General detection for files being created in the Windows startup directory. This could be an indicator of persistence.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Potentially Suspicious Desktop Background Change Via Registry
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects registry value settings that would replace the user's desktop background.
This is a common technique used by malware to change the desktop background to a ransom note or other image.

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: Identifies Maze ransomware in memory or unpacked.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: @bartblaze
Felix Bilstein - yara-signator at cocacoding dot com

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: Detects Maze ransomware payload dll unpacked
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: @VK_Intel

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert http $HOME_NET any -> [92.63.0.0/16,91.218.114.0/24,149.56.245.196] any (msg:"ET MALWARE Maze/ID Ransomware Activity"; flow:established,to_server; urilen:>1; http.method; content:"POST"; http.user_agent; content:"Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1|3b 20|WOW64|3b 20|Trident/7.0|3b 20|AS|3b 20|rv|3a|11.0) like Gecko"; depth:72; endswith; fast_pattern; http.request_body; pcre:"/^[\x20-\x7e\r\n]{0,20}[^\x20-\x7e\r\n]/"; reference:md5,f83fb9ce6a83da58b20685c1d7e1e546; reference:md5,9823800f063a1d4ee7a749961db7540f; classtype:trojan-activity; sid:2027392; rev:6; metadata:affected_product Windows_XP_Vista_7_8_10_Server_32_64_Bit, attack_target Client_Endpoint, created_at 2019_05_29, deployment Perimeter, malware_family Maze_Ransomware, signature_severity Major, tag Ransomware, tag Maze, updated_at 2020_11_17, mitre_tactic_id TA0040, mitre_tactic_name Impact, mitre_technique_id T1486, mitre_technique_name Data_Encrypted_for_Impact;)

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: POLICY-OTHER HTTP request by IPv4 address attempt
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert tcp any any -> any $HTTP_PORTS ( msg:"POLICY-OTHER HTTP request by IPv4 address attempt"; flow:to_server,established; http_header; content:"Host:",fast_pattern,nocase; pcre:"/^Host\x3a\s*(?:(?:25[0-5]|2[0-4][0-9]|[01]?[0-9]?[0-9])\.){3}(?:25[0-5]|2[0-4][0-9]|[01]?[0-9]?[0-9])\s*:?\s*\d*\s*$/ims"; service:http; reference:url,www.w3.org/Protocols/rfc2616/rfc2616-sec15.html; classtype:policy-violation; sid:50447; rev:1; )
